jokes.text: The Big Bopper? Ritchie Valens? Buddy Holly? No! [ALL HISSING]
jokes.setup: Scratchy encounters famous musicians who died in the 1959 plane crash
jokes.punchline: They're all hissing like devils in hell
jokes.explanation: Dark historical reference with cartoon logic - classic Simpsons blend of high and low culture

jokes.text: You get to stay downstairs watching TV with the sound turned way up.
jokes.punchline: Homer's solution for keeping kids occupied during romance
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer parenting - practical but inappropriate

jokes.text: Oh, we'll be upstairs in the bedroom making lovely rope ladders... in case there's a fire.
jokes.setup: Kids ask what parents will be doing
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous euphemism for sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer euphemism - absurd but delivered with confidence

jokes.text: Children, so naive. - What? - Who's naive? I didn't say anything. So naive.
jokes.punchline: Gets caught and continues anyway
jokes.explanation: Homer's inability to stop when caught - good character consistency

jokes.text: Thanks for the love tip, 60 Minutes II.
jokes.setup: Homer is trying to be romantic
jokes.punchline: Credits a news magazine for romance advice
jokes.explanation: Specific cultural reference with absurd application - classic Simpsons

jokes.text: Hey there, Little Red Riding Hood. I ate your granny. And now I'm in the mood for love.
jokes.punchline: Disturbing fairy tale reference as foreplay
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly inappropriate - mixing children's story with seduction

jokes.text: Homer plays lullaby instead of sexy music, Marge falls asleep
jokes.setup: Homer promises his mixtape will get Marge going
jokes.punchline: It's actually a lullaby that puts her to sleep
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer incompetence gag with good setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: Oh, no. That's Maggie's mixtape. Then Maggie must have gotten... [Tom Jones' 'Sex Bomb' playing]
jokes.punchline: Baby has the sexy music
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with absurd role reversal

jokes.text: Shut down on Valentine's Day. That's supposed to be a gimme.
jokes.setup: Homer failed to have sex on Valentine's Day
jokes.punchline: Treats it like a sure thing in sports
jokes.explanation: Perfect sports metaphor for romance - very quotable and relatable

jokes.text: Oh, 'glayvin.' Oh, nice lady android with the true-to-life 'floivic.'
jokes.setup: Frink is presumably with a sex robot
jokes.punchline: His made-up technical words for anatomy
jokes.explanation: Peak Frink - perfectly in character with his scientific gibberish applied to sex

jokes.text: Suicide, eh? That's it. Thank you, outdoor advertising. You've saved my marriage, and not for the first time.
jokes.punchline: Interprets suicide ad as solution and credits billboards for past marital help
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with character consistency - Homer's advertising dependency is a running theme

jokes.text: Oh, yes. I put myself through medical school... dancing under the name Malcolm Sex.
jokes.punchline: Reveal of his stripper name
jokes.explanation: Great character expansion - explains skills with perfect stripper name

jokes.text: Cletus: 'Well, I'm here to win back Brandine. She been making eyes at that photographer what come to document our squalor.'
jokes.setup: Cletus explaining why he's at stripping class
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions photographer documenting their poverty as romantic rival
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus dialogue - self-aware about his poverty while treating it matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Homer, take this quarter, call your mother. Tell her you're never going to be a stripper.
jokes.setup: Homer has used all the oil
jokes.punchline: Harsh assessment delivered with outdated phone cost
jokes.explanation: Classic insult format with period detail (quarter phone call)

jokes.text: Homer: 'Three coats' when asked if he's already oiled, then 'That oil was for the entire class'
jokes.punchline: He used all the oil meant for everyone
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer excess and inconsideration of others

jokes.text: Aren't you gonna chuckle? There's nothing to chuckle about.
jokes.punchline: Homer expects the trademark chuckle, denied
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke - character aware of his own trademark behavior

jokes.text: You, the greasy, naked, bald man. - Ah! You know everything about me.
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks physical description shows deep knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - confusing observation with insight

jokes.text: Unh, the trick is to blot the oil. Wiping just pushes it around. You're preaching to the choir, man.
jokes.punchline: Homer acts like an oil expert
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Homer finding expertise in anything

jokes.text: God, look at you, losers. I can read your minds. Ooh, ooh, I'm afraid of success. It's pizza's fault I'm fat. Oh, I'll stop sucking later.
jokes.punchline: Brutally accurate self-excuses
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about common excuses - very relatable

jokes.text: You see this watch? It's jammed with so many jewels, the hands can't move.
jokes.punchline: Excess makes it non-functional
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - wealth defeating purpose

jokes.text: Well, I drew it on. See?
jokes.punchline: Homer drew a watch on his wrist
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer solution - childlike but sincere

jokes.text: That's a Bentley Mark 12. They gave one to me... one to Steven Spielberg, and they shot the guy who made it.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of murdering craftsman
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from luxury to violence - dark humor

jokes.text: I don't wanna die. I'm so young.
jokes.setup: Reading tip to live each day like your last
jokes.punchline: Homer takes it literally and panics
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal interpretation of metaphor - good character consistency

jokes.text: I was tired of putting my keys in that bowl like a caveman. I finally harnessed the awesome power of the hook.
jokes.punchline: Treats simple technology as breakthrough
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer grandiosity about mundane achievement

jokes.text: I say Phantom Menace sucked more. I say Attack of the Clones sucked more.
jokes.punchline: Debating which bad Star Wars movie was worse
jokes.explanation: Timely cultural reference - both movies were poorly received

jokes.text: Oh, have you now? Well, huzzah, huzzah. I'll just throw back my legs and pollute my britches with delight.
jokes.punchline: Burns' elaborate sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Peak Burns - archaic language with crude imagery, perfectly in character

jokes.text: And what book is that? The Ape Who Wanted a Big Bonus?
jokes.punchline: Burns' dismissive fake title
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns insult - reduces Homer to animal status

jokes.text: Stop wasting my time, you corn-fed man-cow.
jokes.punchline: Elaborate agricultural insult
jokes.explanation: Another great Burns insult - vivid and specific

jokes.text: He just steps over everyone who works for him... taking pleasure in making us feel small. Oh, Homie. Don't let it get you down. So Mr. Burns doesn't take you seriously. Big whoop. Who gives a doodle? Whoopie ding dong doo.
jokes.punchline: Marge's increasingly silly dismissive phrases
jokes.explanation: Marge's attempt at comfort becomes absurd through escalation

jokes.text: So my husband goes to a bar every night. Whoop-de-do. Who gives a bibble? Gabba, gabba, hey.
jokes.punchline: Even more nonsensical phrases, ending with Ramones reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with unexpected punk rock reference

jokes.text: Moe, we've been complaining about him every night for eight years.
jokes.punchline: Homer points out their long-running complaints
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about show's repetitive structure - acknowledges the format

jokes.text: Or give him some face time with sweet lady brick.
jokes.punchline: Poetic euphemism for hitting with brick
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe - violence described poetically

jokes.text: Wait till he steps on this flaming bag of, ah... Lisa's college fund?
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's burning daughter's education money
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Homer's priorities - sacrificing child's future for petty revenge

jokes.text: Heh, talk about your runaway government.
jokes.punchline: Burns' casual political commentary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns understatement - treating monster as bureaucratic problem

jokes.text: The legal owner of this plant would, Canary M. Burns.
jokes.setup: Discussing who would go to jail
jokes.punchline: Plant is legally owned by his pet canary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant corporate satire - rich people avoiding responsibility through absurd legal tricks

jokes.text: Why, Standard Oil was once owned by a half-eaten breakfast.
jokes.punchline: Historically absurd ownership example
jokes.explanation: Surreal but believable corporate history - perfect absurdist escalation

jokes.text: From my room, I can hear everything. Me too. The walls are paper-thin. Ugh. Hi. And it wouldn't hurt you to put up some curtains.
jokes.setup: Marge tells Homer to listen to inner voice
jokes.punchline: Multiple neighbors can hear their private conversation
jokes.explanation: Good escalating reveal of no privacy, ending with Ned's curtain suggestion

jokes.text: Now, fly to the Canary Islands.
jokes.punchline: Pun on destination matching bird type
jokes.explanation: Simple pun but fits Homer's childlike logic

jokes.text: Ah! Good Lord. That canary was supposed to be my pigeon.
jokes.punchline: Mixed metaphor with bird types
jokes.explanation: Good Burns wordplay - mixing patsy/pigeon metaphors

jokes.text: Hello. - Yes, yes, hello. - Now I need to find a patsy. - Hello. You're quite the friendly fellow. - But right now I'm looking for a patsy. - Hello.
jokes.punchline: Homer's persistent hellos prevent Burns from recognizing him as the perfect patsy
jokes.explanation: Great escalating irony - Homer unknowingly prevents his own exploitation

jokes.text: This moon-faced simpleton... is continually interrupting my search for a patsy. Why to...? Hello.
jokes.setup: Burns describing the simpleton interrupting him
jokes.punchline: Realizes the simpleton IS his patsy
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff - Burns' moment of realization

jokes.text: That's right. And as my first act... Mr. Burns, you're fired.
jokes.setup: Homer announced as new plant owner
jokes.punchline: Immediately fires his former boss
jokes.explanation: Satisfying power reversal - classic revenge fantasy

jokes.text: So the caterpillar has emerged from its cocoon... as a shark with a gun for a mouth.
jokes.punchline: Biological metaphor becomes ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Peak Burns - starts with elegant metaphor, becomes beautifully absurd

jokes.text: Eat crowd, old man!
jokes.punchline: Mangles 'eat crow' idiom
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism - close enough to real phrase but wrong

jokes.text: And today begins my reign of... terrific management.
jokes.setup: Homer starting speech like typical villain
jokes.punchline: Pulls back to wholesome leadership
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - subverts expectation of power corruption

jokes.text: Unlike Mr. Burns, I will respect you, the working-class slob... because we are all equals. And now, as I ascend this crystal staircase to my office... I say, avert your gaze.
jokes.punchline: Immediately acts superior and regal
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Homer's instant hypocrisy shows power's immediate corruption

jokes.text: Now, open it again and put a walnut in there.
jokes.punchline: Wants to test door on walnut
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike fascination with power - testing door on random objects

jokes.text: Now I'm forcibly retired, I feel I should give back to society... and do some charitable work. But first, I want to take a lot of opium.
jokes.punchline: Priorities include massive drug use first
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns - noble intentions immediately undermined by vice

jokes.text: Drugs? Everything is drugs. Banana made of drugs, monkey made of drugs. Look, all market made of drugs. Our money is made of drugs.
jokes.punchline: Vendor reveals everything in his country is drugs
jokes.explanation: Surreal escalation - entire economy based on drugs

jokes.text: Homer, I'll go over the year-end profit forecast... if you'll stop looking at my boobs. - No deal.
jokes.setup: Assistant trying to negotiate Homer's attention
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately refuses reasonable request
jokes.explanation: Homer's honest priorities - won't compromise on ogling

jokes.text: Hey, that's a printer port, not a finger hole.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to interact through video monitor
jokes.punchline: Bart corrects his computer confusion
jokes.explanation: Homer's tech confusion - generation gap humor

jokes.text: And I'm the man at the ballpark everyone hates. - The umpire? - No, Billy Crystal.
jokes.punchline: Not umpire but annoying celebrity fan
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with specific cultural knowledge about Crystal's baseball fandom

jokes.text: Burns: 'I worked here for three score and twain.' Homer: 'That's 62 years in the new English.'
jokes.punchline: Homer translating and calling modern English 'new English'
jokes.explanation: Good interplay between Burns' antiquated speech and Homer's interpretation

jokes.text: This was my fiancée, Gertrude. I was working so hard, I missed our wedding, honeymoon... and our divorce proceedings. She died of loneliness. Loneliness and rabies.
jokes.setup: Burns showing photo of lost love
jokes.punchline: Escalating neglect ending with absurd death causes
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from touching to absurd - classic Burns mixture of pathos and ridiculous detail
